question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1.In the first Lok Sabha, the single largest party in the opposition was the Swatantra Party. 2.In the Lok Sabha, a   "Leader of the Opposition" was recognised for the first time in 1969. 3.In the Lok Sabha, if a party does not have a minimum of 75 members, its leader cannot be recognised as the Leader of the Opposition. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1.In the first Lok Sabha... 3.In the Lok Sabha...
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • Statement 1 incorrect: First LS opposition largest was CPI (not Swatantra, formed 1959). • Statement 2 correct: LOP recognised in 1969 (Mavalankar convention formalised). • Statement 3 incorrect: Minimum 10% strength of House (55 members for 543), not 75.

answer_sources.citation: Lok Sabha Secretariat Rules of Procedure, Rule 331A (LOP recognition)

subject: Polity
topic: Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha

question_text_combined: Which of the following leaf modifications occur(s) in the desert areas to inhibit water loss? 1. Hard and waxy leaves 2. Tiny leaves 3. Thorns instead of leaves Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 2 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • All three are xerophytic adaptations: thick waxy cuticle (1), reduced leaf surface (tiny leaves, 2), spines/thorns replace leaves to minimise transpiration (3).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Biology, Ch. 13 (Organisms and Populations)

subject: Environment

question_text_combined: As per the NSSO 70th Round "Situation Assessment Survey of Agricultural Households", consider the following statements: 1. Rajasthan has the highest percentage share of agricultural households among its rural households. 2. Out of the total agricultural households in the country, a little over 60 percent belong to OBCs. 3. In Kerala, a little over 60 percent of agricultural households reported to have received maximum income from sources other than agricultural activities. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 2 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 3 correct per NSSO 70th Round (Rajasthan ~78% agri households; Kerala ~62% non-agri income dominant). • 2 incorrect: OBC share ~45%, not >60%.

answer_sources.citation: NSSO 70th Round Report 'Situation Assessment Survey of Agricultural Households' (2013)
MoSPI Key Indicators of Situation of Agricultural Households in India

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: How is the National Green Tribunal (NGT) different from the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB)? 1. The NGT has been established by an Act whereas the CPCB has been created by an executive order of the Government. 2. The NGT provides environmental justice and helps reduce the burden of litigation in the higher courts whereas the CPCB promotes cleanliness of streams and wells, and aims to improve the quality of air in the country. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. NGT by Act... 2. NGT justice vs CPCB cleanliness
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 incorrect: CPCB established by Water Act 1974 (statutory, not executive). • 2 correct: NGT (2010 Act) for speedy environmental justice; CPCB functions under Water/Air Acts for pollution control.

answer_sources.citation: Water (Prevention & Control of Pollution) Act 1974 Section 3

subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Parliament of India can place a particular law in the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution of India. 2. The validity of a law placed in the Ninth Schedule cannot be examined by any court and no judgement can be made on it. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Parliament Ninth Schedule... 2. Validity cannot be examined
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Parliament can amend & place laws in Ninth Schedule (Art 368). • 2 incorrect: Post-IR Coelho (2007), laws in Ninth Schedule subject to judicial review if violate basic structure.

answer_sources.citation: IR Coelho v. State of Tamil Nadu (2007) 2 SCC 1

subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which one of the following best describes the term "Merchant Discount Rate" sometimes seen in news?
options_combined: (a) The incentive given by a bank... (b) The amount paid back... (c) The charge to a merchant... (d) The incentive given by the Government...
answer_explanation: • MDR is fee charged by banks/payment gateways to merchants for card transactions (debit/credit).

answer_sources.citation: RBI Master Circular on Merchant Acquiring Business

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: What is/are the consequence/consequences of a country becoming the member of the 'Nuclear Suppliers Group'? 1. It will have access to the latest and most efficient nuclear technologies. 2. It automatically becomes a member of  "The Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT)". Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Access to technologies... 2. Automatic NPT member
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: NSG membership enables civil nuclear trade & technology access. • 2 incorrect: NPT membership separate; India not NPT signatory but seeks NSG waiver.
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to India's decision to levy an equalization of 6% on online advertisement services offered by non-resident entities, which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. It is introduced as a part of the Income Tax Act. 2. Non-resident entities that offer advertisement services in India can claim a tax credit in their home country under the    "Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements". Select the correct answer using the code given below:
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Equalisation Levy Income Tax Act... 2. Tax credit DTAA
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Equalisation Levy introduced via Finance Act 2016 (Chapter VIII, not Income Tax Act per se but tax on non-residents). • 2 incorrect: No automatic DTAA credit for Equalisation Levy (treated as tax on business, not covered uniformly).
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Review Committee Report has recommended a debt to GDP ratio of 60% for the general (combined) government by 2023... 2. The Central Government has domestic liabilities of 21% of GDP as compared to that of 49% of GDP of the State Governments. 3. As per the Constitution of India, it is mandatory for a State to take the Central Government's consent for raising any loan if the former owes any outstanding liabilities to the latter. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 3 correct: NK Singh FRBM Review (2017) recommended 60% combined (40% Centre, 20% States) by 2023; Art 293(3) requires Centre consent if State owes to Centre. • 2 incorrect: Actual figures reversed (Centre higher than 49%).
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The quantity of imported edible oils is more than the domestic production of edible oils in the last five years. 2. The Government does not impose any customs duty on all the imported edible oils as a special case. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Imported > domestic edible oils... 2. No customs duty
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: India imports ~60-70% of edible oil needs (palm, soy, sunflower). • 2 incorrect: Customs duty imposed on most (e.g., 7.5-12.5% on crude palm).

answer_sources.citation: Ministry of Commerce Trade Data & DGFT Notifications

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: He wrote biographies of Mazzini, Garibaldi, Shivaji and Shrikrishna; stayed in America for some time; and was also elected to the Central Assembly. He was
options_combined: (a) Aurobindo Ghosh. (b) Bipin Chandra Pal (c) Lala Lajpat Rai (d) Motilal Nehru
answer_explanation: • Lala Lajpat Rai authored biographies of Mazzini, Garibaldi, Shivaji & Krishna; lived in USA (1917-1920); elected to Central Legislative Assembly 1926.

answer_sources.citation: Lala Lajpat Rai Writings & Biographies (Collected Works)
NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 11

subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Aadhaar card can be used as a proof of citizenship or domicile. 2. Once issued, Aadhaar number cannot be deactivated or omitted by the Issuing Authority. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Aadhaar proof citizenship... 2. Cannot be deactivated
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • Both incorrect: Aadhaar not proof of citizenship/domicile (Supreme Court & UIDAI clarification); Aadhaar can be deactivated/omitted on fraud/duplicate grounds (Aadhaar Act Section 23).
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which of the following has/have shrunk immensely/dried up in the recent past due to human activities? 1. Aral Sea 2. Black Sea 3. Lake Baikal Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 (c) 2 only (d) 1 and 3
answer_explanation: • Only Aral Sea drastically shrunk due to Soviet irrigation diversion of Amu & Syr Darya rivers. • Black Sea & Baikal affected but not 'shrunk immensely/dried up'.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Fundamentals of Physical Geography Ch. 5

subject: Geography

question_text_combined: The "Rule of Law Index" is released by which of the following?
options_combined: (a) Amnesty International (b) International Court of Justice (c) The Office of UN Commissioner for Human Rights (d) World Justice Project
answer_explanation: • World Justice Project (WJP) publishes annual Rule of Law Index covering 113+ countries.

answer_sources.citation: World Justice Project Rule of Law Index 2017-18

subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Which one of the following links all the ATMs in India?
options_combined: (a) Indian Banks' Association (b) National Securities Depository Limited (c) National Payments Corporation of India (d) Reserve Bank of India
answer_explanation: • NPCI operates National Financial Switch (NFS) – largest ATM network linking all banks' ATMs in India.
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Capital Adequacy Ratio (CAR) is the amount that banks have to maintain in the form of their own funds to offset any loss that banks incur if the account-holders fail to repay dues. 2. CAR is decided by each individual bank. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. CAR own funds... 2. Decided by individual bank
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: CAR (Basel norm) is bank's capital as % of risk-weighted assets to absorb losses. • 2 incorrect: Prescribed by RBI (minimum 9% + buffers).

answer_sources.citation: RBI Master Circular on Basel III Capital Regulations

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: The identity platform 'Aadhaar' provides open "Application Programming Interfaces (APIs)". What does it imply? 1. It can be integrated into any electronic device. 2. Online authentication using iris is possible. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • Both correct: Aadhaar APIs allow integration in devices/apps; supports biometric (iris/fingerprint) authentication via e-KYC.

answer_sources.citation: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial & Other Subsidies) Act 2016

subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Very recently, in which of the following countries have lakhs of people either suffered from severe famine/acute malnutrition or died due to starvation caused by war/ethnic conflicts?
options_combined: (a) Angola and Zambia (b) Morocco and Tunisia (c) Venezuela and Colombia (d) Yemen and South Sudan
answer_explanation: • Yemen (civil war + Saudi blockade) & South Sudan (civil war/ethnic conflict) faced worst famine crises (UN reports 2017-18).
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Regarding Wood's Dispatch, which of the following statements are true? 1. Grants-in-Aid system was introduced. 2. Establishment of universities was recommended. 3. English as a medium of instruction at all levels of education was recommended. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 2 and 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 2 correct: Wood's Despatch 1854 introduced grants-in-aid & recommended 3 universities (Calcutta, Bombay, Madras). • 3 incorrect: English at higher levels only; vernacular at school level.
subject: History

question_text_combined: With reference to the Parliament of India, which of the following Parliamentary Committees scrutinizes and reports to the House whether the powers to make regulations, rules, sub-rules, by-laws, etc. conferred by the Constitution or delegated by the Parliament are being properly exercised by the Executive within the scope of such delegation?
options_combined: (a) Committee on Government Assurances (b) Committee on Subordinate Legislation (c) Rules Committee (d) Business Advisory Committee
answer_explanation: • Committee on Subordinate Legislation examines whether delegated legislation is within parent Act's scope & reports to House.

answer_sources.citation: Lok Sabha Rules of Procedure Rule 317-320

subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. As per the Right to Education (RTE) Act, to be eligible for appointment as a teacher in a State, a person would be required to possess the minimum qualification laid down by the concerned State Council of Teacher Education. 2. As per the RTE Act, for teaching primary classes, a candidate is required to pass a Teacher Eligibility Test conducted in accordance with the National Council of Teacher Education guidelines. 3. In India, more than 90% of teacher education institutions are directly under the State Governments. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 (d) 3 only
answer_explanation: • 2 correct: RTE Section 23 & NCTE guidelines mandate TET for primary teachers. • 1 incorrect: NCTE (not State Council) lays minimum qualifications. • 3 incorrect: Majority private.
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Tradition — State 1. Chapchar Kut festival — Mizoram 2. Khongjom Parba ballad — Manipur 3. Thang-Ta dance — Sikkim Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 1 and 2 (c) 3 only (d) 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 2 correct: Chapchar Kut (Mizoram spring festival), Khongjom Parba (Manipur ballad on Anglo-Manipur War). • 3 incorrect: Thang-Ta is Manipuri martial dance.

answer_sources.citation: Ministry of Culture Intangible Heritage List
NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III

subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 replaced the Prevention of Food Adulteration Act, 1954. 2. The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) is under the charge of Director General of Health Services in the Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. FSS Act replaced PFA... 2. FSSAI under DGHS
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: FSS Act 2006 consolidated 8 laws including PFA 1954. • 2 incorrect: FSSAI is autonomous statutory body under MoHFW (not under DGHS).

answer_sources.citation: Food Safety and Standards Act 2006 Preamble

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: The term "two-state solution" is sometimes mentioned in the news in the context of the affairs of
options_combined: (a) China (b) Israel (c) Iraq (d) Yemen
answer_explanation: • Two-State Solution refers to Israel-Palestine conflict (independent Palestinian state alongside Israel).

answer_sources.citation: UN Resolutions on Middle East Peace Process

subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to the provisions made under the National Food Security Act, 2013, consider the following statements: 1. The families coming under the category of 'below poverty line (BPL)' only are eligible to receive subsidised food grains. 2. The eldest woman in a household, of age 18 years or above, shall be the head of the household for the purpose of issuance of a ration card. 3. Pregnant women and lactating mothers are entitled to a 'take-home ration' of 1600 calories per day during pregnancy and for six months thereafter. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 (d) 3 only
answer_explanation: • 2 correct: NFSA Section 13 – eldest woman as head for ration card. • 1 & 3 incorrect: Coverage is 75% rural/50% urban (priority + AAY, not just BPL); pregnant/lactating get 600 kcal + protein (not 1600).

answer_sources.citation: National Food Security Act 2013 Sections 3, 13 & Schedule II

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: India enacted The Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999 in order to comply with the obligations to
options_combined: (a) ILO (b) IMF (c) UNCTAD (d) WTO
answer_explanation: • GI Act 1999 to comply with TRIPS Agreement under WTO (Art 22-24 protection of geographical indications).
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. In India, State Governments do not have the power to auction non-coal mines. 2. Andhra Pradesh and Jharkhand do not have gold mines. 3. Rajasthan has iron ore mines. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. States no power non-coal... 3. Rajasthan iron ore
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 (d) 3 only
answer_explanation: • 3 correct: Rajasthan has iron ore (Jaipur, Udaipur). • 1 incorrect: MMDR Amendment 2015 allows States to auction non-coal mines. • 2 incorrect: Jharkhand has gold (Sonali).
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: With reference to digital payments, consider the following statements: 1. BHIM app allows the user to transfer money to anyone with a UPI-enabled bank account. 2. While a chip-pin debit card has four factors of authentication, BHIM app has only two factors of authentication. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: BHIM uses UPI for instant transfers to any UPI-linked account. • 2 incorrect: BHIM uses device + biometric/PIN (2 factors); chip-pin debit also 2-3 factors typically.
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Among the following cities, which one lies on a longitude closest to that of Delhi?
options_combined: (a) Bengaluru (b) Hyderabad (c) Nagpur (d) Pune
answer_explanation: • Delhi ~77° E; Nagpur ~79° E (closest among options).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class VI The Earth Our Habitat Ch. 2

subject: Geography

question_text_combined: International Labour Organization's Conventions 138 and 182 are related to
options_combined: (a) Child labour (b) Adaptation of agricultural practices... (c) Regulation of food prices... (d) Gender parity...
answer_explanation: • ILO C138 (Minimum Age) & C182 (Worst Forms of Child Labour) – core conventions on child labour elimination.
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Regarding Money Bill, which of the following statements is not correct?
options_combined: (a) A bill shall be deemed to be a Money Bill if it contains only provisions relating to imposition... (b) A Money Bill has provisions for the custody... (c) A Money Bill is concerned with the appropriation of moneys out of the Contingency Fund... (d) A Money Bill deals with the regulation of borrowing...
answer_explanation: • Statement c incorrect: Appropriation from Consolidated Fund (Art 114); Contingency Fund under Art 267 (not Money Bill provision).
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: With reference to the election of the President of India, consider the following statements: 1. The value of the vote of each MLA varies from State to State. 2. The value of the vote of MPs of the Lok Sabha is more than the value of the vote of MPs of the Rajya Sabha. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. MLA vote value varies... 2. LS MPs value > RS MPs
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: MLA vote value = State population/1000 ÷ elected MLAs (varies). • 2 incorrect: All MPs (LS & RS) have equal vote value (700).

answer_sources.citation: Presidential & Vice-Presidential Elections Act 1952 & Constitution Art 55

subject: Polity

question_text_combined: In the Indian context, what is the implication of ratifying the 'Additional Protocol' with the 'International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA)'?
options_combined: (a) The civilian nuclear reactors come under IAEA safeguards. (b) The military nuclear installations... (c) The country will have the privilege to buy uranium... (d) The country automatically becomes a member...
answer_explanation: • Additional Protocol (ratified 2014) brings all civilian nuclear facilities under IAEA safeguards (expanded access).
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: Consider the following countries: 1. Australia 2. Canada 3. China 4. India 5. Japan 6. USA Which of the above are among the 'free-trade partners' of ASEAN?
answer_explanation: • ASEAN FTAs with Australia, China, India, Japan (not Canada/USA in listed group).
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to the 'Global Alliance for Climate-Smart Agriculture (GACSA)', which of the following statements is/are correct? 1. GACSA is an outcome of the Climate Summit held in Paris in 2015. 2. Membership of GACSA does not create any binding obligations. 3. India was instrumental in the creation of GACSA. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 2 correct: GACSA voluntary alliance, no binding commitments. • 1 & 3 incorrect: Launched 2014 at FAO (not Paris 2015); India joined later, not instrumental.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Which of the following is/are the aim/aims of "Digital India" Plan of the Government of India? 1. Formation of India's own Internet companies like China did. 2. Establish a policy framework to encourage overseas multinational corporations that collect Big Data to build their large data centres within our national geographical boundaries. 3. Connect many of our villages to the Internet and bring Wi-Fi to many of our schools, public places and major tourist centres. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 3 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • Only 3 is official pillar (Broadband Highways & Digital Infrastructure). 1 & 2 not stated aims.
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Towns sometimes mentioned in news — Country 1. Aleppo — Syria 2. Kirkuk — Yemen 3. Mosul — Palestine 4. Mazar-i-sharif — Afghanistan Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 1 and 4 (c) 2 and 3 (d) 3 and 4
answer_explanation: • 1 & 4 correct: Aleppo (Syria civil war), Mazar-i-Sharif (Afghanistan). • 2 & 3 incorrect: Kirkuk (Iraq), Mosul (Iraq).
subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: In the Federation established by The Government of India Act of 1935, residuary powers were given to the
options_combined: (a) Federal Legislature (b) Governor General (c) Provincial Legislature (d) Provincial Governors
answer_explanation: • Residuary powers vested in Governor-General (Section 104 GoI Act 1935).
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Speaker of the Legislative Assembly shall vacate his/her office if he/she ceases to be a member of the Assembly. 2. Whenever the Legislative Assembly is dissolved, the Speaker shall vacate his/her office immediately. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Speaker vacate if ceases member... 2. Vacate on dissolution
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Art 179(a). • 2 incorrect: Speaker continues till new Assembly elects successor (Art 179 proviso).
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which one of the following reflects the most appropriate relationship between law and liberty?
options_combined: (a) If there are more laws, there is less liberty. (b) If there are no laws, there is no liberty. (c) If there is liberty, laws have to be made by the people. (d) If laws are changed too often, liberty is in danger.
answer_explanation: • Liberty requires law to prevent anarchy (John Locke: where there is no law, there is no freedom).

answer_sources.citation: John Locke Second Treatise of Government Ch. 6

subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. No criminal proceedings shall be instituted against the Governor of a State in any court during his term of office. 2. The emoluments and allowances of the Governor of a State shall not be diminished during his term of office. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. No criminal proceedings Governor... 2. Emoluments not diminished
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • Both correct: Art 361(2) immunity from criminal proceedings; Art 158(3) emoluments not diminished.
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: The well-known painting "Bani Thani" belongs to the
options_combined: (a) Bundi school (b) Jaipur school (c) Kangra school (d) Kishangarh school
answer_explanation: • Bani Thani by Nihal Chand, quintessential Kishangarh school miniature (Radha-inspired).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI An Introduction to Indian Art Ch. 6

subject: History

question_text_combined: What is "Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD)", sometimes seen in the news?
options_combined: (a) An Israeli radar system (b) India's indigenous anti-missile programme (c) An American anti-missile system (d) A defence collaboration between Japan and South Korea
answer_explanation: • THAAD is US Army terminal-phase anti-ballistic missile system (hit-to-kill).

answer_sources.citation: US Missile Defense Agency THAAD Fact Sheet

subject: International Relations

question_text_combined: With reference to cultural history of India, consider the following statements: 1. Most of the Tyagaraja Kritis are devotional songs in praise of Lord Krishna. 2. Tyagaraja created several new ragas. 3. Annamacharya and Tyagaraja are contemporaries. 4. Annamacharya kirtanas are devotional songs in praise of Lord Venkateshwara. Which of the statements given above are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 and 4 only (c) 1, 2 and 3 (d) 2, 3 and 4
answer_explanation: • 2 & 4 correct: Tyagaraja created new ragas; Annamacharya kirtanas to Venkateswara. • 1 incorrect: Tyagaraja to Rama. • 3 incorrect: Annamacharya 15th cent, Tyagaraja 18th-19th.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI An Introduction to Indian Art & Music

subject: History

question_text_combined: Which of the following are regarded as the main features of the "Rule of Law"? 1. Limitation of powers 2. Equality before law 3. People's responsibility to the Government 4. Liberty and civil rights Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 and 4 only (c) 1, 2 and 4 only (d) 1, 2, 3 and 4
answer_explanation: • Dicey’s Rule of Law: supremacy of law, equality, liberty protected (1,2,4). • 3 is opposite (government responsible to people).

answer_sources.citation: A.V. Dicey Introduction to Study of Law of Constitution

subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements correctly describes the meaning of legal tender money?
options_combined: (a) The money which is tendered in courts... (b) The money which a creditor is under compulsion to accept... (c) The bank money... (d) The metallic money...
answer_explanation: • Legal tender: currency creditor must accept in discharge of debt (RBI notes/coins).
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: If a commodity is provided free to the public by the Government, then
options_combined: (a) the opportunity cost is zero. (b) the opportunity cost is ignored. (c) the opportunity cost is transferred from the consumers... (d) the opportunity cost is transferred from the consumers...
answer_explanation: • Opportunity cost borne by taxpayers (resources diverted from other uses).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Introductory Macroeconomics Ch. 2

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Increase in absolute and per capita real GNP do not connote a higher level of economic development, if
options_combined: (a) industrial output fails to keep pace... (b) agricultural output fails... (c) poverty and unemployment increase. (d) imports grow faster than exports.
answer_explanation: • Development requires reduction in poverty/unemployment; mere GNP rise insufficient if inequality rises.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Indian Economic Development Ch. 1

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: Human capital formation as a concept is better explained in terms of a process which enables 1. individuals of a country to accumulate more capital. 2. increasing the knowledge, skill levels and capacities of the people of the country. 3. accumulation of tangible wealth. 4. accumulation of intangible wealth. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 4 (d) 1, 3 and 4
answer_explanation: • Human capital = stock of skills/knowledge (intangible); process increases knowledge & capacities (2 & 4).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Indian Economic Development Ch. 4

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Despite being a high saving economy, capital formation may not result in significant increase in output due to
options_combined: (a) weak administrative machinery (b) illiteracy (c) high population density (d) high capital-output ratio
answer_explanation: • High ICOR means more capital needed per unit output increase (low productivity of capital).
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: After the Santhal Uprising subsided, what was/were the measure/measures taken by the colonial government? 1. The territories called 'Santhal Paraganas' were created. 2. It became illegal for a Santhal to transfer land to a non-Santhal. Select the correct answer using the code given below:
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Santhal Parganas created... 2. Illegal land transfer
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • Both: Santhal Parganas district created 1855; non-transferability of land to non-Santhals to protect tribals.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 10

subject: History

question_text_combined: Economically, one of the results of the British rule in India in the 19th century was the
options_combined: (a) increase in the export of Indian handicrafts (b) growth in the number of Indian owned factories (c) commercialization of Indian agriculture (d) rapid increase in the urban population
answer_explanation: • Commercialisation of agriculture: shift to cash crops for British markets (indigo, cotton, opium).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 10
Bipan Chandra India's Struggle for Independence Ch. 3

subject: History

question_text_combined: If the President of India exercises his power as provided under Article 356 of the Constitution in respect of a particular State, then
options_combined: (a) the Assembly of the State is automatically dissolved. (b) the powers of the Legislature of that State shall be exercisable by or under the authority of the Parliament. (c) Article 19 is suspended in that State. (d) the President can make laws relating to that State.
answer_explanation: • Art 356: President's Rule – State legislative powers vest in Parliament (proclamation).
subject: Polity

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Craft Heritage of 1. Puthukkuli shawls — Tamil Nadu 2. Sujni embroidery — Maharashtra 3. Uppada Jamdani saris — Karnataka Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 1 and 2 (c) 3 only (d) 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Puthukkuli (Toda tribe shawls, TN). • 2 & 3 incorrect: Sujni (Bihar), Uppada Jamdani (Andhra).

answer_sources.citation: Ministry of Textiles GI & Craft List
NCERT Class XI An Introduction to Indian Art

subject: History

question_text_combined: In which of the following areas can GPS technology be used? 1. Mobile phone operations 2. Banking operations 3. Controlling the power grids Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • All three: GPS for location in mobiles, time sync in banking (atomic clock), grid frequency/phasor monitoring.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Reserve Bank of India manages and services Government of India Securities but not any State Government Securities. 2. Treasury bills are issued by the Government of India and there are no treasury bills issued by the State Governments. 3. Treasury bills offer are issued at a discount from the par value. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. RBI manages GoI securities... 3. T-bills discount
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 3 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 2 & 3 correct: Only GoI issues T-bills (91/182/364 days, zero-coupon discount). • 1 incorrect: RBI manages State Development Loans too.

answer_sources.citation: RBI Handbook of Statistics on Indian Economy

subject: Economy

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Earth's magnetic field has reversed every few hundred thousand years. 2. When the Earth was created more than 4000 million years ago, there was 54% oxygen and no carbon dioxide. 3. When living organisms originated, they modified the early atmosphere of the Earth. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 3 correct: Geomagnetic reversals (palaeomagnetic evidence); cyanobacteria oxygenated atmosphere (Great Oxidation Event). • 2 incorrect: Early atmosphere reducing (CO2, methane, no free O2).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Fundamentals of Physical Geography Ch. 2

subject: Geography

question_text_combined: The terms 'WannaCry, Petya and EternalBlue' sometimes mentioned in the news recently are related to
options_combined: (a) Exoplanets (b) Cryptocurrency (c) Cyber attacks (d) Mini satellites
answer_explanation: • WannaCry & Petya ransomware; EternalBlue exploit – all major cyber attacks 2017.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: With reference to the circumstances in Indian agriculture, the concept of "Conservation Agriculture" assumes significance. Which of the following fall under the Conservation Agriculture? 1. Avoiding the monoculture practices 2. Adopting minimum tillage 3. Avoiding the cultivation of plantation crops 4. Using crop residues to cover soil surface 5. Adopting spatial and temporal crop sequencing/crop rotations Select the correct answer using the code given below:
answer_explanation: • Core principles: minimum tillage (2), residue cover (4), crop rotation (5). 1 desirable but not core; 3 not required.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: The term "sixth mass extinction/sixth extinction" is often mentioned in the news in the context of the discussion of
options_combined: (a) Widespread monoculture... (b) Fears of a possible collision... (c) Large scale cultivation of genetically modified... (d) Mankind's over-exploitation/misuse of natural resources...
answer_explanation: • Holocene/Anthropocene extinction driven by human activities: habitat loss, overexploitation, pollution, climate change.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to the Indian Regional Navigation Satellite System (IRNSS), consider the following statements: 1. IRNSS has three satellites in geostationary and four satellites in geosynchronous orbits. 2. IRNSS covers entire India and about 5500 sq. km beyond its borders. 3. India will have its own satellite navigation system with full global coverage by the middle of 2019. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 1 and 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) None
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: NavIC (7 satellites: 3 GEO, 4 GSO). • 2 incorrect: Covers 1500 km beyond borders. • 3 incorrect: Regional system (no global plans by 2019).
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following phenomena: 1. Light is affected by gravity. 2. The Universe is constantly expanding. 3. Matter warps its surrounding space-time. Which of the above is/are the prediction/predictions of Albert Einstein's General Theory of Relativity, often discussed in media?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 & 3 direct GTR predictions (gravitational lensing, curvature). • 2 from Friedmann equations (expanding universe from GTR + observations).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Physics Ch. 15 (optional advanced)

subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: With reference to the Genetically Modified mustard (GM mustard) developed in India, consider the following statements: 1. GM mustard has the genes of a soil bacterium that give the plant the property of pest-resistance to a wide variety of pests. 2. GM mustard has the genes that allow the plant cross-pollination and hybridization. 3. GM mustard has been developed jointly by the IARI and Punjab Agricultural University. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 2 correct: Barnase-barstar-barnase system for hybridisation. • 1 & 3 incorrect: No pest-resistance; developed by Delhi University (Centre for Genetic Manipulation of Crop Plants).

answer_sources.citation: GEAC & MoEFCC GM Mustard Assessment Report

subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Terms sometimes seen in news Context/Topic 1. Belle II experiment — Artificial Intelligence 2. Blockchain technology — Digital/Cryptocurrency 3. CRISPR – Cas9 — Particle Physics Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • Only 2 correct: Blockchain for crypto & distributed ledger. • 1: Belle II particle physics (B-meson). • 3: Gene editing tool.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Which of the following statements best describes "carbon fertilization"?
options_combined: (a) Increased plant growth due to increased concentration of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere (b) Increased temperature... (c) Increased acidity of oceans... (d) Adaptation of all living beings...
answer_explanation: • CO2 fertilization effect: higher atmospheric CO2 enhances photosynthesis & plant growth (under nutrient/water sufficiency).
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: When the alarm of your smartphone rings in the morning... In the context of emerging communication technologies, which one of the following terms best applies to the above scenario?
options_combined: (a) Border Gateway Protocol (b) Internet of Things (c) Internet Protocol (d) Virtual Private Network
answer_explanation: • Interconnected devices communicating & automating via internet – classic IoT.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: With reference to solar power production in India, consider the following statements: 1. India is the third largest in the world in the manufacture of silicon wafers used in photovoltaic units. 2. The solar power tariffs are determined by the Solar Energy Corporation of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Third largest silicon wafers... 2. SECI determines tariffs
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • Both incorrect: India imports most wafers (no major manufacturing rank); tariffs via competitive bidding/CERC, not SECI alone.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: The staple commodities of export by the English East India Company from Bengal in the middle of the 18th century were
options_combined: (a) Raw cotton, oil-seeds and opium (b) Sugar, salt, zinc and lead (c) Copper, silver, gold, spices and tea (d) Cotton, silk, saltpetre and opium
answer_explanation: • Main exports: cotton & silk textiles, saltpetre (gunpowder), opium (to China).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 10
Cambridge Economic History of India Vol. 1

subject: History

question_text_combined: Which one of the following is a very significant aspect of the Champaran Satyagraha?
options_combined: (a) Active all-India participation... (b) Active involvement of Dalit... (c) Joining of peasant unrest to India's National Movement (d) Drastic decrease in the cultivation...
answer_explanation: • First Gandhian mass movement linking peasant grievances (tinkathia) with national freedom struggle.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 13
Bipan Chandra India's Struggle for Independence Ch. 13

subject: History

question_text_combined: Who among the following were the founders of the "Hind Mazdoor Sabha" established in 1948?
options_combined: (a) B. Krishna Pillai... (b) Jayaprakash Narayan... (c) C.P. Ramaswamy Iyer... (d) Ashok Mehta, T.S. Ramanujam and G.G. Mehta
answer_explanation: • Founded by socialist leaders Ashok Mehta, T.S. Ramanujam, G.G. Mehta (split from INTUC/AITUC).

question_text_combined: With reference to the religious practices in India, the "Sthanakvasi" sect belongs to
options_combined: (a) Buddhism (b) Jainism (c) Vaishnavism (d) Shaivism
answer_explanation: • Sthanakvasi is a reformist sect of Jainism (no idols/temples, 17th cent).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Themes in World History Ch. 4

question_text_combined: With reference to the cultural history of India, consider the following statements: 1. White marble was used in making Buland Darwaza and Khankah at Fatehpur Sikri. 2. Red sandstone and marble were used in making Bara Imambara and Rumi Darwaza at Lucknow. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. White marble Fatehpur Sikri... 2. Red sandstone Lucknow
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 only (c) Both 1 and 2 (d) Neither 1 nor 2
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Buland Darwaza & Sheikh Salim Chishti tomb (Khankah) use white marble. • 2 incorrect: Bara Imambara & Rumi Darwaza primarily lakhori bricks & lime plaster.

question_text_combined: Which one of the following foreign travellers elaborately discussed about diamonds and diamond mines of India?
options_combined: (a) Francois Bernier (b) Jean-Baptiste Tavernier (c) Jean de Thevenot (d) Abbe Barthelemy Carre
answer_explanation: • Tavernier (Six Voyages) detailed Golconda diamond mines & trade (17th cent).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part II Ch. 5

question_text_combined: With reference to Indian history, who among the following is a future Buddha, yet to come to save the world?
options_combined: (a) Avalokiteshvara (b) Lokesvara (c) Maitreya (d) Padmapani
answer_explanation: • Maitreya (Pali Metteyya) – future Buddha in Mahayana & Theravada tradition.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Themes in World History Ch. 4

question_text_combined: Which one of the following statements does not apply to the system of Subsidiary Alliance introduced by Lord Wellesley?
options_combined: (a) To maintain a large standing army at other's expense (b) To keep India safe from Napoleonic danger (c) To secure a fixed income for the Company (d) To establish British paramountcy over the Indian States
answer_explanation: • Subsidiary Alliance aimed at political control & military (a,b,d); not primarily fixed income (subsidy for troops, but not core).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 5

subject: History

question_text_combined: Which of the following led to the introduction of English Education in India? 1. Charter Act of 1813 2. General Committee of Public Instruction, 1823 3. Orientalist and Anglicist Controversy Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • All three: Charter Act §43 allocated funds; Committee 1823; controversy led to Macaulay's Minute 1835.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class VIII Our Pasts III Ch. 8

subject: History

question_text_combined: Which one of the following is an artificial lake?
options_combined: (a) Kodaikanal (Tamil Nadu) (b) Kolleru (Andhra Pradesh) (c) Nainital (Uttarakhand) (d) Renuka (Himachal Pradesh)
answer_explanation: • Kodaikanal Lake man-made (British era, 1863). Others natural (Kolleru lagoon, Nainital tectonic, Renuka glacial).

question_text_combined: With reference to Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana, consider the following statements: 1. It is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Labour and Employment. 2. It, among other things, will also impart training in soft skills, entrepreneurship, financial and digital literacy. 3. It aims to align the competencies of the unregulated workforce of the country to the National Skill Qualification Framework. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 3 only (b) 2 only (c) 2 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 2 & 3 correct: PMKVY (MSDE flagship) includes soft skills & NSQF alignment. • 1 incorrect: Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship.
subject: Economy

question_text_combined: In 1920, which of the following changed its name to "Swarajya Sabha"?
options_combined: (a) All India Home Rule League (b) Hindu Mahasabha (c) South Indian Liberal Federation (d) The Servants of India Society
answer_explanation: • Annie Besant's Home Rule League renamed Swarajya Sabha in 1920 to align with Congress.

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XII Themes in Indian History Part III Ch. 13
Bipan Chandra India's Struggle for Independence

question_text_combined: Which among the following events happened earliest?
options_combined: (a) Swami Dayanand established Arya Samaj. (b) Dinabandhu Mitra wrote Neeldarpan. (c) Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay wrote Anandmath. (d) Satyendranath Tagore became the first Indian to succeed in the Indian Civil Services Examination.
answer_explanation: • Neeldarpan 1858-59 (indigo revolt). Arya Samaj 1875; Anandmath 1882; Satyendranath ICS 1863.

subject: History

question_text_combined: Which of the following is/are the possible consequence/s of heavy sand mining in riverbeds? 1. Decreased salinity in the river 2. Pollution of groundwater 3. Lowering of the water-table Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • 2 & 3: Sand mining lowers water table, causes saline intrusion (pollution), not decreased salinity.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to agricultural soils, consider the following statements: 1. A high content of organic matter in soil drastically reduces its water holding capacity. 2. Soil does not play any role in the sulphur cycle. 3. Irrigation over a period of time can contribute to the salinization of some agricultural lands. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Organic matter reduces WHC... 3. Irrigation salinization
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • Only 3 correct: Over-irrigation + poor drainage causes secondary salinization. • 1 incorrect (organic matter increases WHC). • 2 incorrect (soil microbes in S-cycle).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class XI Geography Fundamentals Ch. 6

subject: Environment

question_text_combined: The Partnership for Action on Green Economy (PAGE), a UN mechanism to assist countries transition towards greener and more inclusive economies, emerged at
options_combined: (a) The Earth Summit on Sustainable Development 2002, Johannesburg (b) The United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development 2012, Rio de Janeiro (c) The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change 2015, Paris (d) The World Sustainable Development Summit 2016, New Delhi
answer_explanation: • PAGE launched at Rio+20 (2012 UN Conference on Sustainable Development).
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: "3D printing" has applications in which of the following? 1. Preparation of confectionery items 2. Manufacture of bionic ears 3. Automotive industry 4. Reconstructive surgeries 5. Data processing technologies Select the correct answer using the code given below:
options_combined: (a) 1, 3 and 4 only (b) 2, 3 and 5 only (c) 1 and 4 only (d) 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5
answer_explanation: • All five demonstrated: food printing, bionic organs, auto parts, medical implants, rapid prototyping in data/tech.
subject: Science & Tech

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. The Barren Island volcano is an active volcano located in the Indian territory. 2. Barren Island lies about 140 km east of Great Nicobar. 3. The last time the Barren Island volcano erupted was in 1991 and it has remained inactive since then. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 (c) 3 only (d) 1 and 3
answer_explanation: • 1 correct: Only active volcano in India (Andaman). • 2 incorrect (~135 km NE of Port Blair, not Great Nicobar). • 3 incorrect: Erupted 2017 (ongoing activity).
subject: Geography

question_text_combined: Why is a plant called Prosopis juliflora often mentioned in news?
options_combined: (a) Its extract is widely used in cosmetics. (b) It tends to reduce the biodiversity in the area in which it grows. (c) Its extract is used in the synthesis of pesticides. (d) None of the above
answer_explanation: • Invasive species (Vilayati kikar) in arid areas; outcompetes native flora, reduces biodiversity.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: Consider the following statements: 1. Most of the world's coral reefs are in tropical waters. 2. More than one-third of the world's coral reefs are located in the territories of Australia, Indonesia and Philippines. 3. Coral reefs host far more number of animal phyla than those hosted by tropical rainforests. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
raw_blocks_combined: 1. Tropical waters... 3. More phyla than rainforests
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 only (b) 3 only (c) 1 and 3 only (d) 1, 2 and 3
answer_explanation: • All correct: Coral reefs 0-30° latitude; Australia/Indonesia/Philippines ~35-40%; highest marine biodiversity (32/34 phyla vs rainforests 10).
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: "Momentum for Change: Climate Neutral Now" is an initiative launched by
options_combined: (a) The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (b) The UNEP Secretariat (c) The UNFCCC Secretariat (d) The World Meteorological Organization
answer_explanation: • UNFCCC initiative (2015) for climate neutrality & voluntary commitments.
subject: Environment

question_text_combined: With reference to educational institutions during colonial rule in India, consider the following pairs: Institution Founder 1. Sanskrit College at Benaras — William Jones 2. Calcutta Madarsa — Warren Hastings 3. Fort William College — Arthur Wellesley Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
options_combined: (a) 1 and 2 (b) 2 only (c) 1 and 3 (d) 3 only
answer_explanation: • Only 2 correct: Calcutta Madrasa 1781 by Warren Hastings. • 1: Jonathan Duncan; 3: Lord Wellesley (not Arthur).

answer_sources.citation: NCERT Class VIII Our Pasts III Ch. 8

subject: History

question_text_combined: Consider the following pairs: Regions sometimes mentioned in news Country 1. Catalonia — Spain 2. Crimea — Hungary 3. Mindanao — Philippines 4. Oromia — Nigeria Which of the pairs given above are correctly matched?

answer_explanation: • 1 & 3 correct: Catalonia independence (Spain), Mindanao conflict (Philippines). • 2 Crimea (Ukraine/Russia), 4 Oromia (Ethiopia).

question_text_combined: Consider the following events: 1. The first democratically elected communist party government formed in a State in India. 2. India's then largest bank, 'Imperial Bank of India', was renamed 'State Bank of India'. 3. Air India was nationalised and became the national carrier. 4. Goa became a part of independent India. Which of the following is the correct chronological sequence of the above events?

question_text_combined: Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of Right to Life and Personal Liberty. Which of the following in the Constitution of India correctly and appropriately imply the above statement?
options_combined: (a) Article 14 and the provisions under the 42nd Amendment... (b) Article 17 and the Directive Principles... (c) Article 21 and the freedoms guaranteed in Part III (d) Article 24 and the provisions under the 44th Amendment...

question_text_combined: In which one of the following States is Pakhui Wildlife Sanctuary located?

question_text_combined: With reference to India's satellite launch vehicles, consider the following statements: 1. PSLVs launch the satellites useful for Earth resources monitoring whereas GSLVs are designed mainly to launch communication satellites. 2. Satellites launched by PSLV appear to remain permanently fixed in the same position in the sky, as viewed from a particular location on Earth. 3. GSLV Mk III is a four-staged launch vehicle with the first and third stages using solid rocket motors; and the second and fourth stages using liquid rocket engines. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

options_combined: (a) 1 only (b) 2 and 3 (c) 1 and 2 (d) 3 only

question_text_combined: Consider the following: 1. Birds 2. Dust blowing 3. Rain 4. Wind blowing Which of the above spread plant diseases?

question_text_combined: With reference to organic farming in India, consider the following statements: 1.
